title: cerastium ligusticum subsp. ligusticum
intro: Cerastium ligusticum subsp. ligusticum, also known as Mountain Whitlowgrass, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects, making it a popular remedy for various health conditions. The health benefits of Cerastium ligusticum subsp. ligusticum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations. The plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried and used in capsules or as a topical application.
